title: lupinus montanus subsp. glabrior
intro: Lupinus montanus subsp. glabrior is a subspecies of the lupin plant, a flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lupinus montanus subsp. glabrior include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its benefits.
